item.title: ICT to deliver Abu Sayeed murder verdict today
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-2 is scheduled to deliver its verdict today in the crimes against humanity case over the murder of Begum Rokeya University, Rangpur student Abu Sayeed, the first martyr of the July 2024 uprising......

item.title: Illegal parking of buses causes suffering
item.description: Passengers and other road users are suffering immensely in the capital Dhaka due to traffic congestion caused by the parking of inter-district buses on busy roads and illegal bus counters across the city......

item.title: Govt to import another 3 lakh tonnes of diesel
item.description: The government on Wednesday decided to buy three lakh tonnes of diesel to meet the emergency need, as the high demand for fuel oils continues resulting in overcrowded filling stations, particularly in Dhaka city......

item.title: 10 more die with measles symptoms
item.description: At least 10 more children died with suspected measles symptoms and 1,248 others were infected across Bangladesh in the 24 hours until 8:00am on Wednesday......

item.title: India agrees to return suspected killers of Hadi: govt
item.description: India on Wednesday agreed to return the suspected killers of Inquilab Mancha convener Shaheed Osman Hadi, who were arrested there, to Bangladesh in accordance with the extradition treaty between the two countries, according to a statement from Bangladesh’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs......

item.title: JS passes bill empowering govt to ban entities
item.description: Jatiya Sangsad on Wednesday passed 13 more bills, including the Anti-Terrorism (Amendment) Bill ratifying an ordinance that empowered the governemnt to ban any entity on terrorism charges and under which the interim government banned activities of the Awami League......

item.title: US alleges Iran’s 10-point peace plan not same set of agreed conditions
item.description: Tehran said Wednesday its 10-point plan for securing an end to the war with the United States would require Washington to accept its uranium enrichment programme and the lifting of all sanctions......

item.title: World welcomes US-Iran ceasefire, urges lasting peace in ME
item.description: The United States and Iran have agreed to a two-week ceasefire, with talks to finalise a peace deal set to begin in Pakistan’s Islamabad on Friday, reports AL Jazeera on Wednesday......

item.title: Protesters halt Kushtia bridge toll resumption plan
item.description: Students, transport workers and local residents halted a government initiative to resume toll collection on the Syed Mashud Rumi Bridge at Kumarkhali in Kushtia on Tuesday, forcing officials to leave the site without completing their inspection......

item.title: War may push 12 lakh into poverty in Bangladesh: WB
item.description: The World Bank on Wednesday warned that the ongoing Middle East war could push an additional 12 lakh people in Bangladesh into poverty this year, undermining the expected recovery and intensifying economic strain......Human rights reports

item.title: Experts suggest building strategic energy reserve
item.description: Experts focused on energy sourcing and the importance of building a strategic reserve, describing it as one of the most effective tools to mitigate Bangladesh’s energy challenges......

item.title: PMI decreases in March
item.description: The country’s Purchasing Managers’ Index in March witnessed a decrease by 2.2 points to 53.5 from the previous month, indicating a slower growth, according to the latest PMI report published jointly by the Metropolitan Chamber of Commerce and Industry and Policy Exchange Bangladesh......

item.title: NBR rejects REHAB’s bid to use undisclosed money
item.description: National Board of Revenue chairman has rejected proposal of the Real Estate and Housing Association of Bangladesh to allow use of undisclosed money in housing sector by paying low tax......

item.title: Bangladesh, UK agree to reactivate trade dialogue
item.description: Bangladesh and the United Kingdom reached a consensus to reactivate the bilateral Trade and Investment Dialogue, aiming to further consolidate economic ties and explore new avenues for strategic cooperation......

item.title: Stocks charge up on ME war ceasefire optimism
item.description: Stocks on Wednesday witnessed a strong resurgence as the announcement of a two-week ceasefire deal between Iran and the US sparked optimism across the trading floor, triggering renewed accumulation of the beaten-down scrips in anticipation of improved market momentum amid easing geopolitical concerns......

item.title: KSA keen to boost cooperation in industrial, port sectors
item.description: Saudi Arabia has expressed eagerness to work with Bangladesh for strengthening strategic cooperation in seaport development, investment expansion and the industrial sector......

item.title: Factors for improving non-life insurance market
item.description: THE non-life insurance market in Bangladesh currently comprises a number of companies that significantly exceeds prevailing market demand. This imbalance has intensified competition within the sector, and in some instances has led to practices that may be considered irregular or inconsistent with ethical standards. Such behaviour has contributed to a negative public......

item.title: Memory, war and the battle for Bangladesh’s soul
item.description: I WRITE not only as a citizen of Bangladesh, but as the granddaughter of Kazi Nuruzzaman — a Mukti Bahini sector commander who did not inherit history, but helped forge it in fire......

item.title: Renewable energy stagnation
item.description: WARS travel far and away. But, they do not arrive with tanks or missiles but with invoices. Somewhere in the Strait of Hormuz or the Levant, a tanker hesitates, insurance premiums rise and within weeks a country thousands of miles away starts rationing fuel, recalculates subsidies and quietly revises the arithmetic of survival. Bangladesh now lives through such a quiet invasion of costs......Diaspora community news

item.title: Socio-financial impact of data breach
item.description: THE largest supermarket chain Shwapno, which has more than four million registered consumers, has said that its customer database was breached towards the end of March and cybercriminals demanded $1.5 million, which comes to more than Tk 183 million. The chain shop made the disclosure after its consumer details — names, phone numbers and purchase......

item.title: Time for India to reconsider hostile border policy
item.description: IN AN inconsiderate move, India is reportedly contemplating the deployment of reptiles along unfenced river borders with Bangladesh. India’s Border Security Force leaders on March 26 directed the field units to explore the feasibility of using reptiles such as snakes and crocodiles in river stretches along the Bangladesh border to prevent unauthorised movement of people......

item.title: Govt must commit to judicial independence
item.description: THE government’s asking 24 lower court judges to explain their remarks on social media opposing the repeal of the ordinances related to the judiciary, especially the Supreme Court secretariat, lays bare the fragility of judicial independence. That the judges have been called on by the executive to explain their remarks to the very executive is precisely the kind of......City & Local Guides

item.title: US-Iran truce hangs in balance as war flares in Lebanon
item.description: The fragile two-week truce between Iran and the United States was hanging in the balance on Wednesday, with Tehran threatening to resume hostilities as Israel launched a major bombardment of Lebanon....

item.title: Dominant PSG leave Liverpool right up against it in Champions League tie
item.description: Desire Doue and Khvicha Kvaratskhelia scored the goals to give a dominant Paris Saint-Germain a 2-0 win over Liverpool in their Champions League quarter-final first leg on Wednesday, as the holders seized a clear advantage to take to Anfield for next week's return....

item.title: Atletico punish 10-man Barca, take control of Champions League tie
item.description: Julian Alvarez and Alexander Sorloth's goals earned Atletico Madrid a commanding 2-0 lead over 10-man Barcelona in a tense Champions League quarter-final first leg clash on Wednesday....

item.title: Turning ordinances into laws demanded
item.description: Rights activists and family members of victims of enforced disappearance on Wednesday urged the government to immediately turn the Enforced Disappearance Prevention and Remedies Ordinance, 2025 and the National Human Rights Commission Ordinance, 2025 into laws......

item.title: Scrap ‘unequal’ trade deal with US govt: discussion
item.description: Politicians, economists and journalists on Wednesday at a discussion in the capital Dhaka called on the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government to take immediate steps to scrap the trade deal signed with the United States during the rule of the interim government......

item.title: British delegation meets UGC chairman
item.description: The United Kingdom trade envoy to Bangladesh Baroness Rosie Winterton of Doncaster has proposed simplifying the policy to strengthen joint ventures and international cooperation in the higher education sector in Bangladesh......

item.title: 12th death anniversary of ABM Musa today
item.description: The 12th death anniversary of veteran journalist ABM Musa will be observed today with due respect......

item.title: CAB protests at ‘artificial’ edible oil crisis, calls for urgent action
item.description: Consumer rights body Consumers Association of Bangladesh on Wednesday formed a human chain in front of the Jatiya Press Club protesting what it called an artificial shortage of edible oil in the market and placed a five-point demand to resolve the crisis......

item.title: IUB holds seminar on measles awareness
item.description: The department of public health of Independent University, Bangladesh organised a seminar titled Outbreak Alert: Protecting Our Community from Measles at the DMK Lecture Gallery of IUB in Dhaka on Wednesday......

item.title: Hajj Office, Parjatan Corporation sign memo
item.description: The Hajj Office and the Bangladesh Parjatan Corporation signed a memorandum of understanding at the corporation office in Dhaka on Wednesday......

item.title: Online class with participation of foreign faculty inaugurated at BMU
item.description: Vice-chancellor of the Bangladesh Medical University Professor FM Siddiqui inaugurated the regular online class programme with participation of foreign faculty in the clinical oncology department of the university in Dhaka on Wednesday......

item.title: British Council Women in STEM scholarships open to applicants
item.description: The British Council is offering fully funded scholarships to women to study science, technology, engineering and mathematics courses in the United Kingdom......

item.title: Two held with 1.72 lakh Yaba in Cox’s Bazar
item.description: The para-military force Border Guard Bangladesh has detained two suspected drug smugglers along with 1,72,200 pieces of Yaba tablets concealed inside a fish-laden pickup truck at Shilkhali check post under Ukhia Battalion......

item.title: 60,295 teacher posts vacant in MPO schools, colleges: minister
item.description: Education minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon on Wednesday told parliament that 60,295 posts of teachers were vacant in MPO-listed schools and colleges across the country then......

item.title: 12 slaughtered horses recovered in Munshiganj
item.description: Police recovered 12 slaughtered horses and rescued one sick horse from an abandoned house at Bhoberchar in Gazaria upazila of Munshiganj early Wednesday, creating panic among local residents......

item.title: NU signs deal with ACCA
item.description: The National University has signed a memorandum of understanding with the Association of Chartered Certified Accountants in Dhaka on Tuesday......

item.title: Court seizes Asaduzzaman Noor’s tax files
item.description: A Dhaka court on Wednesday ordered the seizure of tax files of former cultural affairs minister Asaduzzaman Noor......

item.title: Kyiv calls for Ukraine ceasefire after Iran truce
item.description: Kyiv on Wednesday called on the United States to pressure Russia into ending its invasion of Ukraine, saying Washington’s ceasefire agreement with Iran showed the success of US ‘decisiveness.’......

item.title: 11 illegal warehouses of soya bean oil found in Sirajganj
item.description: A mobile court on Wednesday fined an edible oil dealer Tk 90,000 for hoarding, supply irregularities and conditional sales in Sirajganj......

item.title: Yunus calls on world leaders for decisive action to end ME conflict
item.description: Noble laureate Professor Muhammad Yunus on Wednesday called on world leaders to take immediate and decisive action to end the ongoing conflict in the Middle East......

item.title: Habiganj SAD leader Mahadi alleges BBC Bangla report distresses his family
item.description: Habiganj district unit member secretary of Student Against Discrimination Mahdi Hasan alleged that a BBC Bangla report, which he called ‘baseless’ seriously distressed his and his family’s life......

item.title: Two workers killed in Chattogram warehouse roof collapse
item.description: Two workers were killed after a tin-shed roof had collapsed on them while they were doing welding at a warehouse at Sitakunda upazila in Chattogram on Wednesday......

item.title: Muslim world urged to oppose Myanmar junta
item.description: A Rohingya rights campaigner spearheading a genocide complaint filed in Indonesia against Myanmar’s next president urged the Muslim world on Wednesday to oppose the junta accused of the abuses......

item.title: Afghanistan, Pakistan agree to avoid escalation: China
item.description: Pakistan and Afghanistan have agreed to avoid any escalation in their armed conflict, during talks hosted by China in recent days, Beijing said Wednesday......

item.title: MPs engage in debate over NHRC autonomy
item.description: Jatiya Sangsad witnessed a heated debate on Wednesday over the independence of the National Human Rights Commission following the tabling of the July Mass Uprising (Protection and Liability Determination) Bill 2026 by home minister Salahuddin Ahmed......

item.title: Preparations under way for different progs to welcome Bengali New Year
item.description: Preparations for various events, including traditional processions and cultural shows, to welcome the Bengali New Year 1433 at different venues across the capital Dhaka are under way, resonating hopes for a new beginning......
